questions.text: Two charges are placed at a certain distance apart in vacuum. If an insulating slab is placed between them, then force will
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Incorrect as per condition. As; F= (​1/4πε0​εr)​.(q1​q2​​/r2)

When an insulating slab, also known as a dielectric, is placed between two charges, the force between them will generally decrease. This is because the dielectric reduces the effective electric field between the charges due to its polarization. The Coulomb force between the charges is inversely proportional to the permittivity of the medium between them. When a dielectric with a relative permittivity is introduced, As, F= (​1/4πε0​εr)​.(q1​q2​​/r2)
Incorrect as per condition. As; F= (​1/4πε0​εr)​.(q1​q2​​/r2)
Incorrect as per condition. As; F= (​1/4πε0​εr)​.(q1​q2​​/r2)

questions.explanation: When an insulating slab, also known as a dielectric, is placed between two charges, the force between them will generally decrease. This is because the dielectric reduces the effective electric field between the charges due to its polarization. The Coulomb force between the charges is inversely proportional to the permittivity of the medium between them. When a dielectric with a relative permittivity is introduced, As, F= (​1/4πε0​εr)​.(q1​q2​​/r2)
hints: insulating slab is placed between them

questions.text: Potential inside a hollow charged spherical conductor:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The electric potential inside a hollow charged spherical conductor is constant. This is because the electric field inside a hollow conductor is zero, and therefore, no work is done in moving a charge from one point to another inside the conductor. As a result, the potential difference between any two points inside the conductor is zero, and the potential throughout the interior is uniform and equal to the potential on the surface.As;V=Q/4πϵ0​rwhere ( ϵ0 ) is the vacuum permittivity. This potential is the same at all points inside the hollow spherical conductor.

options.text: varies directly as distance from centre
options.explanation: Incorrect as per condition that the electric potential inside a hollow charged spherical conductor is constant.

options.text: varies directly as square of distance from the centre
options.explanation: Incorrect as per condition that the electric potential inside a hollow charged spherical conductor is constant.

options.text: varies inversely as distance from centre
options.explanation: Incorrect as per condition that the electric potential inside a hollow charged spherical conductor is constant.

questions.explanation: The electric potential inside a hollow charged spherical conductor is constant. This is because the electric field inside a hollow conductor is zero, and therefore, no work is done in moving a charge from one point to another inside the conductor. As a result, the potential difference between any two points inside the conductor is zero, and the potential throughout the interior is uniform and equal to the potential on the surface.As;V=Q/4πϵ0​rwhere ( ϵ0 ) is the vacuum permittivity. This potential is the same at all points inside the hollow spherical conductor.

questions.text: When the hot and cold junctions of a thermocouple are interchanged, the thermo- emf:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Incorrect as per condition.
Incorrect as per condition.
Incorrect as per condition.

When the hot and cold junctions of a thermocouple are interchanged, the thermo-electromotive force (EMF) changes sign. This means that if the EMF was initially positive, it will become negative, and vice versa. The magnitude of the EMF remains the same, but the direction of the current flow is reversed due to the change in the temperature gradient direction. So, the correct answer is that the thermo-EMF changes sign.

questions.explanation: When the hot and cold junctions of a thermocouple are interchanged, the thermo-electromotive force (EMF) changes sign. This means that if the EMF was initially positive, it will become negative, and vice versa. The magnitude of the EMF remains the same, but the direction of the current flow is reversed due to the change in the temperature gradient direction. So, the correct answer is that the thermo-EMF changes sign.
hints: hot and cold junctions of a thermocouple are interchanged

questions.text: If the resistivity of a conductor is 2×10- 6 Ω-m then its conductivity is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Incorrect as per formula; σ=1/ρ​
The conductivity of a material can be calculated as the reciprocal of its resistivity.σ=1/ρ​ =1/2×10−6Ω⋅m​ =5×105S/mSo, the conductivity is (5×105) Siemens per meter (S/m) or (Ω-1m-1).
Incorrect as per formula; σ=1/ρ​
Incorrect as per formula; σ=1/ρ​

questions.text: The source of emf transfers its maximum power to the external circuit when ( r = internal resistance and R = load resistance):
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Incorrect as per Maximum Power Transfer Theorem.

The condition for maximum power transfer from a source of emf to an external circuit occurs when the load resistance ( R ) is equal to the internal resistance ( r ) of the source. This is known as the Maximum Power Transfer Theorem, and it can be mathematically expressed as:R=rWhen this condition is met, the power transferred to the load is maximized.
Incorrect as per Maximum Power Transfer Theorem.
Incorrect as per Maximum Power Transfer Theorem.

questions.explanation: The condition for maximum power transfer from a source of emf to an external circuit occurs when the load resistance ( R ) is equal to the internal resistance ( r ) of the source. This is known as the Maximum Power Transfer Theorem, and it can be mathematically expressed as:R=rWhen this condition is met, the power transferred to the load is maximized.

questions.text: A galvanometer is made sensitive by:
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: using a small and thick suspension wire
options.explanation: It is incorrect as per formula;I=cθ/​NAB​

options.text: decreasing the area of the coil
options.explanation: It is incorrect as per formula;I=cθ/​NAB​

The formula for the current sensitivity of a galvanometer is given by:I=cθ/​NAB​So, it can enhance the galvanometer’s ability to detect smaller currents by increasing its current sensitivity, which is the deflection per unit current produced by the galvanometer.

options.text: reducing the number of turns of the coil

questions.text: A coil of 150 loops is pulled in 0.06s from poles of the magnet, which decreases the magnetic flux linked with the coil from 6×10-4 Wb to 2×10-4 Wb. The average emf induced in the coil is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: We can use Faraday’s law of induction; emf=−N(ΔΦ/Δt​)The change in magnetic flux (ΔΦ) is:ΔΦ=Φfinal​−Φinitia ​= (2×10−4 Wb)−(6×10−4 Wb) = −4×10−4 WbNow, we can calculate the average emf:emf=−150×(−4×10−4 Wb) / 0.06 s ​=1 VSo, the average emf induced in the coil is 1 V.
Incorrect as per formula; emf=−N(ΔΦ/Δt​)
Incorrect as per formula; emf=−N(ΔΦ/Δt​)
Incorrect as per formula; emf=−N(ΔΦ/Δt​)

questions.explanation: We can use Faraday’s law of induction; emf=−N(ΔΦ/Δt​)The change in magnetic flux (ΔΦ) is:ΔΦ=Φfinal​−Φinitia ​= (2×10−4 Wb)−(6×10−4 Wb) = −4×10−4 WbNow, we can calculate the average emf:emf=−150×(−4×10−4 Wb) / 0.06 s ​=1 VSo, the average emf induced in the coil is 1 V.

questions.text: The quantity that remains constant in a transformer is
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: According to principle, it is not possible for current to remain constant.
According to principle, it is not possible for voltage to remain constant.
According to principle, it is not possible for resistance to remain constant.

The power remains constant. This means that the power input to the primary coil is equal to the power output from the secondary coil, minus any losses due to inefficiency. The formula for power ((P)) is given by (P = VI), where (V) is voltage and (I) is current. So, if the voltage increases during the transformation, the current decreases proportionally, and vice versa, to keep the power constant. This is a consequence of the conservation of energy principle. Pprimary = Psecondary

questions.explanation: The power remains constant. This means that the power input to the primary coil is equal to the power output from the secondary coil, minus any losses due to inefficiency. The formula for power ((P)) is given by (P = VI), where (V) is voltage and (I) is current. So, if the voltage increases during the transformation, the current decreases proportionally, and vice versa, to keep the power constant. This is a consequence of the conservation of energy principle. Pprimary = Psecondary

questions.text: The minimum number of diodes required for full wave rectification are:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: In this condition full wave rectification is not possible.

For full wave rectification, the minimum number of diodes required is two (This is for a center-tapped full wave rectifier configuration).However, if you’re referring to a full wave bridge rectifier, then four diodes are needed.
In this condition full wave rectification is not possible.

questions.explanation: For full wave rectification, the minimum number of diodes required is two (This is for a center-tapped full wave rectifier configuration).However, if you’re referring to a full wave bridge rectifier, then four diodes are needed.

questions.text: A force of 500 N is applied to one end of a cylindrical steel rod of diameter 50cm, the tensile stress is;
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Incorrect as per formula; σ= F/A​
Incorrect as per formula; σ= F/A​

We use the formula: σ= F/A​Given:Force (F) = 500 NDiameter of the rod = 50 cm = 0.5 mThe cross-sectional area of a cylinder is calculated using the formula for the area of a circle ( A = πr2 ), where ( r ) is the radius of the circle.The radius is half of the diameter, so:r =0.5m/2 =0.25mNow, we can calculate the area:A = π(0.25)2 = π(0.0625 = 0.19635m² (rounded to 5 decimal places)Finally, we can calculate the tensile stress: σ = 500/0.19635 = 2546.48 Nm-2So, the tensile stress is approximately 2546.48 or 2.5×103Nm- 2.
Incorrect as per formula; σ= F/A​

questions.explanation: We use the formula: σ= F/A​Given:Force (F) = 500 NDiameter of the rod = 50 cm = 0.5 mThe cross-sectional area of a cylinder is calculated using the formula for the area of a circle ( A = πr2 ), where ( r ) is the radius of the circle.The radius is half of the diameter, so:r =0.5m/2 =0.25mNow, we can calculate the area:A = π(0.25)2 = π(0.0625 = 0.19635m² (rounded to 5 decimal places)Finally, we can calculate the tensile stress: σ = 500/0.19635 = 2546.48 Nm-2So, the tensile stress is approximately 2546.48 or 2.5×103Nm- 2.

questions.text: The radius of 10th orbit in hydrogen atom is;
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Incorrect as per formula; rn​=r0​×n2
Incorrect as per formula; rn​=r0​×n2

To solve for the radius of the 10th orbit in a hydrogen atom in nanometers (nm), we’ll use the Bohr model formula: rn​=r0​×n2where ( r0 ) is the Bohr radius, approximately ( 0.529×10-10 ) meters, and ( n ) is the principal quantum number (orbit number). For the 10th orbit:r10​=0.529×10−10×102 metersr10​=5.29 nmSo, the radius of the 10th orbit in a hydrogen atom is 5.29 nm.
Incorrect as per formula; rn​=r0​×n2

questions.explanation: To solve for the radius of the 10th orbit in a hydrogen atom in nanometers (nm), we’ll use the Bohr model formula: rn​=r0​×n2where ( r0 ) is the Bohr radius, approximately ( 0.529×10-10 ) meters, and ( n ) is the principal quantum number (orbit number). For the 10th orbit:r10​=0.529×10−10×102 metersr10​=5.29 nmSo, the radius of the 10th orbit in a hydrogen atom is 5.29 nm.

questions.text: Which phenomenon does NOT verify particle nature of light?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This phenomenon verify the particle nature of light.
This phenomenon verify the particle nature of light.
This phenomenon verify the particle nature of light.
The phenomenon that does not verify the particle nature of light is diffraction.

questions.explanation: The phenomenon that does not verify the particle nature of light is diffraction. Diffraction is the bending of light waves around the corners of an obstacle or aperture, which is a behavior characteristic of waves. The other phenomena —Compton scattering, the photoelectric effect, and pair production—are all consistent with the particle nature of light, as they involve interactions with photons as discrete packets of energy.
